mission.title: opentelemetry-operator: Support sidecar injection in another pod namespace different from the otel colletor instance.
mission.description: Support sidecar injection in another pod namespace different from the otel colletor instance.. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current opentelemetry-operator deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentelemetry-operator version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
helm list -n opentelemetry-operator 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentelemetry-operator installation.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-operator configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-operator configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-operator
```
If I have an OpenTelemetry collector on sidecar mode in one namespace (eg, test1) but want a pod on other namespace (test2) to be injected, this is not actually possible with the opentelemetry-operator.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support sidecar injection in another pod namespace…
steps.description:
>If operator service account has a cluster role to update/patch pods - it should work right.

The operator is certainly able to inject, the concern is whether it should just trust what the user requested, or if it should do the authorization. This is a concern for multi-tenant environments, where
```yaml
kubectl apply -n test1 -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1alpha1
kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
metadata:
  name: simplest
spec:
  mode: sidecar
  config: |
    receivers:
      jaeger:
        protocols:
          grpc:
    processors:
      queued_retry:

    exporters:
      logging:

    service:
      pipelines:
        traces:
          receivers: [jaeger]
          processors: [queued_retry]
          exporters: [logging]
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-operator
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-operator --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support sidecar injection in another pod namespace…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
>If operator service account has a cluster role to update/patch pods - it should work right.

The operator is certainly able to inject, the concern is whether it should just trust what the user requested, or if it should do the authorization. This is a concern for multi-tenant environments, where each tenant is located on their own namespace.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -n test1 -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1alpha1
kind: OpenTelemetryCollector
metadata:
  name: simplest
spec:
  mode: sidecar
  config: |
    receivers:
      jaeger:
        protocols:
          grpc:
    processors:
      queued_retry:

    exporters:
      logging:

    service:
      pipelines:
        traces:
          receivers: [jaeger]
          processors: [queued_retry]
          exporters: [logging]
EOF

kubectl apply -n test1 -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: myapp
  labels:
    app: myapp
  annotations:
    sidecar.opentelemetry.io/inject: "true"
spec:
  containers:
  - name: myapp
    image: jaegertracing/vertx-create-span:operator-e2e-tests
    ports:
      - containerPort: 8080
        protocol: TCP
EOF

kubectl apply -n test1 -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: myapp
  labels:
    app: myapp
  annotations:
    sidecar.opentelemetry.io/inject: "true"
spec:
  containers:
  - name: myapp
    image: jaegertracing/vertx-create-span:operator-e2e-tests
    ports:
      - containerPort: 8080
        protocol: TCP
EOF

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-operator installed or the issue environment reproducible.
